steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures needing connection
steps.content: The sentence describes two actions of the researchers: seeking to find connections and analyzing posts. Both involve the same subject and must be expressed in one sentence.

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining multiple verbs of the same subject
steps.content:
A participle (hoping) turns the purpose into a modifier: “a team of researchers hoping to find connections... analyzed geotagged social media posts.” The other options (hoped, was hoping, were hoping) would create a finite-verb sequence without coordination before “analyzed,” leaving the sentence ungrammatical.

That confirms B is correct.
